item.title:
US OFFERS $10M BOUNTY FOR INFORMATION ON IRANIAN LEADERS
The U.S. Department of State is offering up to $10 million for information on senior officials tied to Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, according to the Rewards for Justice.
Those sought include Mojtaba Khamenei, Ali Asghar Hejazi, and Esmail Khatib, along with other senior defense and IRGC figures.
item.description:
US OFFERS $10M BOUNTY FOR INFORMATION ON IRANIAN LEADERS

The U.S. Department of State is offering up to $10 million for information on senior officials tied to Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, according to the Rewards for Justice.

Those sought include Mojtaba Khamenei, Ali Asghar Hejazi, and Esmail Khatib, along with other senior defense and IRGC figures.

item.title: ERDOGAN: TURKEY WILL NOT GET DRAGGED INTO IRAN WAR, BUT IS PREPARED AGAINST ALL THREATS
item.description: ERDOGAN: TURKEY WILL NOT GET DRAGGED INTO IRAN WAR, BUT IS PREPARED AGAINST ALL THREATS

item.title: 🚨 AMBREY SAYS MERCHANT VESSEL REPORTED HEARING SOUND OF EXPLOSION AT UNDETERMINED DISTANCE FROM THE VESSEL, APPROXIMATELY IN UAE'S AL SHARJAH ANCHORAGE
item.description: 🚨 AMBREY SAYS MERCHANT VESSEL REPORTED HEARING SOUND OF EXPLOSION AT UNDETERMINED DISTANCE FROM THE VESSEL, APPROXIMATELY IN UAE'S AL SHARJAH ANCHORAGE

item.title:
MAMDANI PROPOSES SLASHING NY ESTATE TAX EXEMPTION TO $750K
Zohran Mamdani wants to cut New York’s estate tax exemption from over $7 million to $750,000 and raise the top rate from 16% to 50% to help close a $5.4 billion city budget gap.
The plan, part of a memo to state lawmakers, would make New York’s estate tax the lowest exemption in the U.S., far below other states like Oregon ($1 million) or Connecticut ($14 million).
Experts warn high estate and income taxes could drive wealthy residents away, reducing revenue. The proposal is unlikely to pass this year, as neither the legislature nor Governor Kathy Hochul included it in their budget plans.
item.description:
MAMDANI PROPOSES SLASHING NY ESTATE TAX EXEMPTION TO $750K

Zohran Mamdani wants to cut New York’s estate tax exemption from over $7 million to $750,000 and raise the top rate from 16% to 50% to help close a $5.4 billion city budget gap.

The plan, part of a memo to state lawmakers, would make New York’s estate tax the lowest exemption in the U.S., far below other states like Oregon ($1 million) or Connecticut ($14 million).

Experts warn high estate and income taxes could drive wealthy residents away, reducing revenue. The proposal is unlikely to pass this year, as neither the legislature nor Governor Kathy Hochul included it in their budget plans.

item.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guards: We Are Now Carrying Out The Most Powerful Attack On Israel Since The Start Of The War - Tasnim
item.description: Iranian Revolutionary Guards: We Are Now Carrying Out The Most Powerful Attack On Israel Since The Start Of The War - Tasnim

item.title: CRUDE FUTURES TURN POSITIVE AS STRAIT OF HORMUZ REMAINS CLOSED BY WAR
item.description: CRUDE FUTURES TURN POSITIVE AS STRAIT OF HORMUZ REMAINS CLOSED BY WAR

item.title:
BOFA: STOCK MARKET NEAR LEVEL THAT COULD SPUR POLICY ACTION
Michael Hartnett warned that if the S&P 500 drops further—around 6,600, just 1% below recent levels—it could trigger a policy response from the White House or the Federal Reserve.
Hartnett cited soaring oil prices, a protracted Iran war, and global instability as catalysts. He also flagged overbought assets like gold, semiconductors, and European stocks, while oversold sectors such as software, bank loans, and bitcoin may stabilize once policymakers act. Potential interventions could include easing tariffs, de-escalating the conflict, or Fed stimulus measures like rate cuts or bond purchases.
item.description:
BOFA: STOCK MARKET NEAR LEVEL THAT COULD SPUR POLICY ACTION

Michael Hartnett warned that if the S&P 500 drops further—around 6,600, just 1% below recent levels—it could trigger a policy response from the White House or the Federal Reserve.

Hartnett cited soaring oil prices, a protracted Iran war, and global instability as catalysts. He also flagged overbought assets like gold, semiconductors, and European stocks, while oversold sectors such as software, bank loans, and bitcoin may stabilize once policymakers act. Potential interventions could include easing tariffs, de-escalating the conflict, or Fed stimulus measures like rate cuts or bond purchases.

item.title: 🚨 IRAN GIVES APPROVAL TO INDIAN GOVERNMENT FOR TWO LIQUEFIED PETROLEUM TANKERS TO SAIL THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ – SOURCES

item.title: ELON MUSK HAS ORDERED ANOTHER ROUND OF JOB CUTS AT XAI - FT
item.description: ELON MUSK HAS ORDERED ANOTHER ROUND OF JOB CUTS AT XAI - FT

item.title:
IRAN WAR DISRUPTS ZINC SUPPLIES TO CHINA
China’s zinc smelters face raw-material shortages after shipments from Iran were halted amid the ongoing conflict, compounding delays from Russia’s new Ozernoye mine.
Iran and Russia together supply about 15% of China’s zinc feedstock. Port closures at Bandar Abbas in the Strait of Hormuz and canceled cargoes from Oman have tightened supplies, forcing Chinese buyers to seek alternatives for May and June.
Industry analysts warn the conflict could ripple through other commodities markets, with aluminum, nickel, copper, and fertilizers already seeing price pressures.
item.description:
IRAN WAR DISRUPTS ZINC SUPPLIES TO CHINA

China’s zinc smelters face raw-material shortages after shipments from Iran were halted amid the ongoing conflict, compounding delays from Russia’s new Ozernoye mine.

Iran and Russia together supply about 15% of China’s zinc feedstock. Port closures at Bandar Abbas in the Strait of Hormuz and canceled cargoes from Oman have tightened supplies, forcing Chinese buyers to seek alternatives for May and June.

Industry analysts warn the conflict could ripple through other commodities markets, with aluminum, nickel, copper, and fertilizers already seeing price pressures.

item.title: BARCLAYS EXPECTS US FED TO DELIVER TWO 25 BPS RATE CUTS EACH IN SEPTEMBER 2026 AND MARCH 2027 VS PRIOR FORECAST OF CUTS IN JUNE AND SEPTEMBER THIS YEAR
item.description: BARCLAYS EXPECTS US FED TO DELIVER TWO 25 BPS RATE CUTS EACH IN SEPTEMBER 2026 AND MARCH 2027 VS PRIOR FORECAST OF CUTS IN JUNE AND SEPTEMBER THIS YEAR

item.title:
US MONITORS IRANIANS AT HOME AMID TERROR CONCERNS
The U.S. is keeping some Iranian residents “under watch” over concerns about potential domestic terrorism tied to the ongoing conflict with Iran, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News Radio interview.
U.S. agencies have elevated counterterror alerts and are monitoring possible sleeper‑cell threats on U.S. soil as tensions rise following U.S. and allied strikes on Iranian targets and fears of retaliation.
item.description:
US MONITORS IRANIANS AT HOME AMID TERROR CONCERNS

The U.S. is keeping some Iranian residents “under watch” over concerns about potential domestic terrorism tied to the ongoing conflict with Iran, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News Radio interview.

U.S. agencies have elevated counterterror alerts and are monitoring possible sleeper‑cell threats on U.S. soil as tensions rise following U.S. and allied strikes on Iranian targets and fears of retaliation.

item.title:
TRUMP SUGGESTS HIS NATIONAL SECURITY TEAM DOESN’T DISAGREE MUCH: “I CONVINCE THEM ALL”
Donald Trump told Fox News that while he allows his national security team to voice opinions, differences are minimal because “I convince them all to let’s do it my way.”
item.description:
TRUMP SUGGESTS HIS NATIONAL SECURITY TEAM DOESN’T DISAGREE MUCH: “I CONVINCE THEM ALL”

Donald Trump told Fox News that while he allows his national security team to voice opinions, differences are minimal because “I convince them all to let’s do it my way.”

item.title:
TRUMP SAYS IRAN WAR WILL END WHEN HE FEELS IT “IN MY BONES”
Donald Trump told The Brian Kilmeade Show he will know the Iran conflict is over “when I feel it in my bones,” while suggesting it may not last much longer.
U.S. and Israeli military operations against Iran have continued since February 28, with Trump giving varied signals on the war’s duration.
item.description:
TRUMP SAYS IRAN WAR WILL END WHEN HE FEELS IT “IN MY BONES”

Donald Trump told The Brian Kilmeade Show he will know the Iran conflict is over “when I feel it in my bones,” while suggesting it may not last much longer.

U.S. and Israeli military operations against Iran have continued since February 28, with Trump giving varied signals on the war’s duration.

item.title:
GOLDMAN RAISES BRENT FORECAST ABOVE $100 AMID HORMUZ CRISIS
Goldman Sachs now expects Brent crude to average over $100 a barrel in March and $85 in April due to the Iran war and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.
Brent futures briefly hit $119.50 recently, the highest since mid-2022. Goldman predicts prices may ease to the low $70s later this year but warns prolonged disruptions could push fourth-quarter Brent to $93 a barrel, up from a previous $71 forecast.
item.description:
GOLDMAN RAISES BRENT FORECAST ABOVE $100 AMID HORMUZ CRISIS

Goldman Sachs now expects Brent crude to average over $100 a barrel in March and $85 in April due to the Iran war and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

Brent futures briefly hit $119.50 recently, the highest since mid-2022. Goldman predicts prices may ease to the low $70s later this year but warns prolonged disruptions could push fourth-quarter Brent to $93 a barrel, up from a previous $71 forecast.

RECESSION ODDS SURGE TO 36% 📈
Kalshi shows the probability of a U.S. recession this year has jumped nearly 15 points in weeks, now at 36%. https://kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnber/recession/kxrecssnber-26?utm_source=walterbloomberg

item.title:
POWELL EXPECTED TO WARN OF STAGFLATION RISKS
Ahead of the March Fed meeting, Jerome Powell is likely to acknowledge stagflation risks as oil prices surge, according to Bank of America.
The Summary of Economic Projections is expected to show higher headline and core inflation, with longer-run growth and rate projections ticking up, while Powell signals a cautious, wait-and-see approach.
item.description:
POWELL EXPECTED TO WARN OF STAGFLATION RISKS

Ahead of the March Fed meeting, Jerome Powell is likely to acknowledge stagflation risks as oil prices surge, according to Bank of America.

The Summary of Economic Projections is expected to show higher headline and core inflation, with longer-run growth and rate projections ticking up, while Powell signals a cautious, wait-and-see approach.

item.title:
GAS PRICES JUMP IN COLORADO AMID IRAN OIL CRUNCH
Gas prices in parts of Colorado, including the Denver area, are climbing toward about $3.89 per gallon, according to gas price expert Patrick De Haan — a move linked to higher oil prices from the ongoing Iran‑related crisis and disruptions to global supply
item.description:

item.title:
JPMORGAN: OIL SUPPLY SHUT-INS MAY HIT 12M BPD
Global oil production shut-ins could surge to 12 million barrels per day next week as shipping disruptions continue, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co..
Head of commodities research Natasha Kaneva said the massive supply gap means the market can only rebalance through lower consumption.
However, she stressed this isn’t traditional “demand destruction.” Consumers still want fuels like diesel, jet fuel and LPG—but supply shortages mean they simply can’t get them.
item.description:
JPMORGAN: OIL SUPPLY SHUT-INS MAY HIT 12M BPD

Global oil production shut-ins could surge to 12 million barrels per day next week as shipping disruptions continue, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co..

Head of commodities research Natasha Kaneva said the massive supply gap means the market can only rebalance through lower consumption.

However, she stressed this isn’t traditional “demand destruction.” Consumers still want fuels like diesel, jet fuel and LPG—but supply shortages mean they simply can’t get them.

item.title: TRUMP TELLS FOX HE BELIEVES IRAN LEADER IS ALIVE BUT 'DAMAGED
item.description: TRUMP TELLS FOX HE BELIEVES IRAN LEADER IS ALIVE BUT 'DAMAGED

item.title:
EUROPE OPENS TALKS WITH IRAN OVER HORMUZ SHIPPING
France and other European nations have begun tentative talks with Iran to secure safe passage for their ships through the Strait of Hormuz, the Financial Times reported.
Italy has also tried to open discussions with Tehran as officials grow concerned about disruptions to the key energy route while Brent crude trades near $100 a barrel.
Officials cautioned there is no guarantee the talks will progress or that Iran is willing to negotiate.
item.description:
EUROPE OPENS TALKS WITH IRAN OVER HORMUZ SHIPPING

France and other European nations have begun tentative talks with Iran to secure safe passage for their ships through the Strait of Hormuz, the Financial Times reported.

Italy has also tried to open discussions with Tehran as officials grow concerned about disruptions to the key energy route while Brent crude trades near $100 a barrel.

Officials cautioned there is no guarantee the talks will progress or that Iran is willing to negotiate.

item.title: CRAMER: 'NOT BUYING THE 2007 SCENARIO'
item.description: CRAMER: 'NOT BUYING THE 2007 SCENARIO'

item.title:
IRAN ALLOWS TURKISH SHIP THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ
Iran has allowed a Turkish-owned ship to pass through the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations, according to a Turkish minister.
Transport Minister Abdulkadir Uraloğlu said the vessel was permitted to transit because it had used an Iranian port.
He added that 14 more Turkish ships remain in the area, while India has also held talks with Iran to secure safe passage for its vessels.
item.description:
IRAN ALLOWS TURKISH SHIP THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Iran has allowed a Turkish-owned ship to pass through the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations, according to a Turkish minister.

Transport Minister Abdulkadir Uraloğlu said the vessel was permitted to transit because it had used an Iranian port.

He added that 14 more Turkish ships remain in the area, while India has also held talks with Iran to secure safe passage for its vessels.

item.title:
🚨 SAUDI ARABIA SLASHES OIL OUTPUT AMID HORMUZ DISRUPTION
Saudi Arabia has cut oil production by about 20% to roughly 8 million barrels per day after shutting two major offshore fields during the regional conflict, sources told Reuters.
The cuts follow the disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran. The shutdown of the Safaniya and Zuluf fields—together producing over 2 million bpd—drove the decline.
State oil giant Saudi Aramco declined to comment.
According to the International Energy Agency, Gulf producers including Iraq, Qatar, Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia have collectively cut at least 10 million bpd, with further losses likely if shipping disruptions continue.
item.description:
🚨 SAUDI ARABIA SLASHES OIL OUTPUT AMID HORMUZ DISRUPTION

Saudi Arabia has cut oil production by about 20% to roughly 8 million barrels per day after shutting two major offshore fields during the regional conflict, sources told Reuters.

The cuts follow the disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran. The shutdown of the Safaniya and Zuluf fields—together producing over 2 million bpd—drove the decline.

State oil giant Saudi Aramco declined to comment.

According to the International Energy Agency, Gulf producers including Iraq, Qatar, Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia have collectively cut at least 10 million bpd, with further losses likely if shipping disruptions continue.

item.title: 🚨🚨 SAUDI ARABIA CUTS OIL OUTPUT BY AT LEAST 2 MILLION BPD TO AROUND 8 MLN BPD, SOURCES SAY
item.description: 🚨🚨 SAUDI ARABIA CUTS OIL OUTPUT BY AT LEAST 2 MILLION BPD TO AROUND 8 MLN BPD, SOURCES SAY

item.title:
RBC WARNS IRAN WAR COULD SEND OIL ABOVE RECORD HIGHS
The conflict between the U.S. and Iran could last far longer than expected, potentially pushing oil prices past previous crisis highs, according to RBC Capital Markets.
Commodity strategist Helima Croft said the bank now believes the war could stretch into spring due to expanded U.S. objectives and Iran’s asymmetric military capabilities.
RBC estimates oil could surpass the 2022 peak of $128 per barrel during the Russia-Ukraine War if fighting continues for several more weeks. If the war drags on for months, prices could exceed the 2008 record of $146 a barrel.
Croft also warned Iran’s large stock of drones, missiles and naval mines could sustain a prolonged “war of attrition,” increasing upward pressure on crude prices.
item.description:
RBC WARNS IRAN WAR COULD SEND OIL ABOVE RECORD HIGHS

The conflict between the U.S. and Iran could last far longer than expected, potentially pushing oil prices past previous crisis highs, according to RBC Capital Markets.

Commodity strategist Helima Croft said the bank now believes the war could stretch into spring due to expanded U.S. objectives and Iran’s asymmetric military capabilities.

RBC estimates oil could surpass the 2022 peak of $128 per barrel during the Russia-Ukraine War if fighting continues for several more weeks. If the war drags on for months, prices could exceed the 2008 record of $146 a barrel.

Croft also warned Iran’s large stock of drones, missiles and naval mines could sustain a prolonged “war of attrition,” increasing upward pressure on crude prices.

item.description: ITALY'S INDUSTRY MINISTER : CRISIS IN MIDDLE EAST RISKS CAUSING SUPPLY DISRUPTIONS FOR CRITICAL RAW MATERIALS

item.title:
TRUMP WARNS OF HARD STRIKE ON IRAN, SAYS U.S. MAY ESCORT SHIPS
President Donald Trump said the United States could escort vessels through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary and warned that Iran would be hit “very hard” in the coming week.
Speaking in an interview with Fox & Friends and Brian Kilmeade, Trump said the U.S. would protect oil tankers passing through the critical shipping route if required but hopes tensions will ease.
His remarks come as the Strait of Hormuz remains a key focus during the ongoing U.S.–Israel war with Iran-backed forces, with oil prices nearing $100 per barrel amid growing market fears.
item.description:
TRUMP WARNS OF HARD STRIKE ON IRAN, SAYS U.S. MAY ESCORT SHIPS

President Donald Trump said the United States could escort vessels through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary and warned that Iran would be hit “very hard” in the coming week.

Speaking in an interview with Fox & Friends and Brian Kilmeade, Trump said the U.S. would protect oil tankers passing through the critical shipping route if required but hopes tensions will ease.

His remarks come as the Strait of Hormuz remains a key focus during the ongoing U.S.–Israel war with Iran-backed forces, with oil prices nearing $100 per barrel amid growing market fears.

item.title: 🚨 FRANCE AND ITALY OPEN TALKS WITH IRAN IN HOPE OF SECURING SAFE HORMUZ PASSAGE - FT
item.description: 🚨 FRANCE AND ITALY OPEN TALKS WITH IRAN IN HOPE OF SECURING SAFE HORMUZ PASSAGE - FT
